accept: act help workflow OR a new act help markers topic includes a section on commit_marker invariants.

Section explains: format, source (short_id), why doctor's grep keeps working as the unique prefix grows, and what to do if you build a marker by hand (use show --commit-marker, do not slice the id yourself).
act help help topic listing surfaces the markers topic if added as separate.
payload.description: Sub-agent #2 (act-5467) report: 'the relationship between short_id (variable length, min 4 hex, ids.ShortestUniquePrefixes) and doctor's hardcoded 8-char id[:8] slice... they happen to agree for the minimum case but doctor does not actually use ShortestUniquePrefixes; it just slices id[:8]. Implementing [the act-5467 acceptance criterion] meant trusting that show's already-computed short_id is the right thing.' This is the one piece of system-coherence the agent had to reverse-engineer from three files. Suggested 4-line note in act help (likely act help workflow or a new act help markers topic) explaining: format (act-XXXX); origin (cli.ShortestUniquePrefixes via show.short_id, min 4 hex); doctor still matches because it greps for the literal string and longer prefixes contain the 4-char prefix as a substring.
payload.title: act help should explain commit_marker invariants (short_id vs doctor slice)
